title: Mitrephora lanotan
intro: Mitrephora lanotan is a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae, commonly known as the lanotan tree. The plant has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Mitrephora lanotan include annonaceous acetogenins, alkaloids, and other compounds that have been shown to exhibit strong antioxidant and cytotoxic activities. In traditional herbal medicine, Mitrephora lanotan is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is commonly used to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal problems, as well as to boost the immune system.
taxonomy: Mitrephora lanotan is classified within the plant family Annonaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the custard apple family. This family comprises over 2,000 species, many of which are found in tropical regions of the world. Mitrephora lanotan is specifically a genus of flowering plants native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. The classification of Mitrephora lanotan within the Annonaceae family reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in this diverse group.
